Minimap.name: className
Minimap.description: Optional extra className applied to the outer container.

Minimap.name: maxSize
Minimap.description:
Maximum size for the minimap thumbnail (CSS pixels). The thumbnail
preserves the page aspect ratio so the actual dimensions are at most
`maxSize` on the longer axis. Defaults to `200`.

Zoom.name: className
Zoom.description: Optional extra className applied to the outer container.

LayerList.name: title
LayerList.description:
Optional title rendered above the list. Pass an empty string to omit
the header entirely (useful when the surrounding shell already labels
the panel).

LayerList.Row.name: index
LayerList.Row.description: Display index in the visible (z-desc) list.

LayerList.Row.name: readOnly
LayerList.Row.description: When `true`, drag-reorder and the hide/lock toggles are suppressed.

LayerList.Row.name: onMoveRow
LayerList.Row.description:
Reorder callback fired when a row is dragged over another row's hover
midpoint, mirroring the Pages primitive's behavior.

LayerRow.name: index
LayerRow.description: Display index in the visible (z-desc) list.

LayerRow.name: readOnly
LayerRow.description: When `true`, drag-reorder and the hide/lock toggles are suppressed.

LayerRow.name: onMoveRow
LayerRow.description:
Reorder callback fired when a row is dragged over another row's hover
midpoint, mirroring the Pages primitive's behavior.

DrawingCanvas.name: finalScale
DrawingCanvas.description: Display scale applied by Canvas (pageScale * zoom).

useSectionDrag.name: groupDrag
useSectionDrag.type: { size: number; onStart: () => void; onMove: (dx: number, dy: number) => void; onEnd: () => void; } | undefined
useSectionDrag.description:
Group drag hook (Phase 16). When provided and `groupDrag.size > 1`, the
section's individual `onUpdate` is bypassed in favour of
`groupDrag.onMove(dx, dy)` so the whole selection translates together.

Coordinates are in canvas display pixels (matching the input movement),
so the receiver converts to PDF points itself.

useImageCrop.name: scale
useImageCrop.description:
Display scale: `finalScale` from {@link useEditorSection }. Pointer deltas
arrive in display pixels; the hook divides by `scale` before applying
them to the crop region (which lives in PDF points).

SectionCropOverlay.name: scale
SectionCropOverlay.description:
Display scale (`finalScale`) — point values are multiplied by this
to render in display pixels so the overlay aligns with the visible
section box.

Toaster.name: toastOptions
Toaster.description:
Override the default toast styling. Defaults to a theme-token-styled
card matching `bg-card` / `text-card-foreground`. Anything you pass
deep-merges into the bundled defaults, so consumers only need to set the
fields they want to change.

Ruler.name: className
Ruler.description: Optional extra className applied to the outer container.

Ruler.name: unit
Ruler.description:
Override the display unit. Falls back to `display.rulerUnit` from the
editor context (defaults to `'pt'`).

TemplateGallery.name: templates
TemplateGallery.description: Templates to render — order is preserved.

TemplateGallery.name: onTemplateSelected
TemplateGallery.description:
Fired after the template has been applied via the editor's
`UPDATE_DOCUMENT` action. Useful for analytics or closing a dialog.

TemplateGallery.name: className
TemplateGallery.description: Optional className wrapping the gallery grid.

KeybindingHelp.name: keymap
KeybindingHelp.description:
Override the displayed keymap. Defaults to {@link DEFAULT_KEYMAP}. Pass
the same partial map you handed to `Editor.Root` `keybindings` so the
dialog reflects the actual active bindings.

Anything omitted falls back to the default — the merge mirrors what the
runtime layer does.

KeybindingHelp.name: open
KeybindingHelp.description:
Externally control the open state. When omitted, the dialog manages its
own visibility and listens for the `showHelp` keybinding to toggle.

KeybindingHelp.name: onOpenChange
KeybindingHelp.description: Called when the user requests open/close.

Toolbar.name: children
Toolbar.description:
Optional children. When provided, the toolbar renders the inverse
default layout and shows whatever children the caller passes.
When omitted, falls back to the bundled default layout — the same
arrangement the editor has always shipped with.

AddShapeButton.name: shape
AddShapeButton.description: Which shape primitive to insert. Defaults to `'rect'`.

AddShapeButton.name: label
AddShapeButton.description: Override the displayed label. Defaults to "Add Rectangle/Circle/Line".

PropertiesPanel.name: children
PropertiesPanel.description:
Optional children. When provided, the panel renders them as the body —
useful for custom compositions that swap sub-sections or add their own
fields. When omitted, the panel falls back to the bundled default
arrangement: `LayoutSection`, type-specific section (Text / Shape),
then `LayerSection`. An empty selection always renders the
{@link EmptyState} regardless of children.

PageBackground.name: pageIndex
PageBackground.description:
Page to target. Defaults to the document's current page (1-based
index, converted to 0-based internally to match the reducer action).

Canvas.name: children
Canvas.description:
Two kinds of children are supported:

1. **Section template** — a single non-overlay child rendered once per
   section. Pass `<Editor.Section />` (or a custom section component
   that consumes {@link useEditorSection }) to control how each section
   is rendered. Defaults to the bundled `<Section />` when omitted.
2. **Canvas overlays** — primitives that opt in via the
   `__editorCanvasOverlay` marker (e.g. {@link Editor.Zoom }). These are
   rendered once, anchored to the canvas viewport, and have access to
   {@link useEditorCanvas } via context. Any number can be supplied
   alongside the section template.

Canvas.name: readOnly
Canvas.description:
Force the canvas into read-only mode regardless of the root's
`readOnly` flag. Defaults to `false`. The effective read-only state is
`root.readOnly || canvas.readOnly` — either one is enough to suppress
drag, resize, drop, file upload, and section-toolbar buttons.

Set automatically by {@link Editor.StaticCanvas }.

StaticCanvas.name: children
StaticCanvas.description:
Two kinds of children are supported:

1. **Section template** — a single non-overlay child rendered once per
   section. Pass `<Editor.Section />` (or a custom section component
   that consumes {@link useEditorSection }) to control how each section
   is rendered. Defaults to the bundled `<Section />` when omitted.
2. **Canvas overlays** — primitives that opt in via the
   `__editorCanvasOverlay` marker (e.g. {@link Editor.Zoom }). These are
   rendered once, anchored to the canvas viewport, and have access to
   {@link useEditorCanvas } via context. Any number can be supplied
   alongside the section template.

useCanvasZoom.name: step
useCanvasZoom.description: Step applied by zoom in/out buttons (defaults to 0.1).

useCanvasZoom.name: onZoomChange
useCanvasZoom.description: Optional callback fired whenever zoom changes (e.g. for analytics).

Properties.name: children
Properties.description:
Optional children. When provided, the properties bar renders an empty
shell with the children inside. When omitted, falls back to the bundled
default layout (document name + page size + orientation, plus a mobile
settings sheet).

PageSizeSelect.name: fullWidth
PageSizeSelect.description: When true, expands the trigger to fill its container (mobile sheet).

OrientationSelect.name: fullWidth
OrientationSelect.description: When true, expands the trigger to fill its container (mobile sheet).

BrushWeightSlider.name: value
BrushWeightSlider.description: Active brush weight in PDF points (1–20).

BrushWeightSlider.name: onChange
BrushWeightSlider.description: Fired when the user moves the slider.

BrushWeightSlider.name: previewColor
BrushWeightSlider.description: Optional preview color used by the swatch. Defaults to current weight color.

usePdfGeneration.name: document
usePdfGeneration.description: Current editor document. Read at call time of download/print.

usePdfGeneration.name: onSizeKnown
usePdfGeneration.description: Called with the generated blob's size once download completes.

usePdfGeneration.name: backend
usePdfGeneration.description:
Optional PDF backend override. Defaults to `@docmosaic/core` exports.
Use this to swap the bundled jsPDF path for a custom implementation.

Root.name: pdf
keybindings
Root.description:
Keyboard shortcuts.

- Omit (default): the built-in keymap is active — `mod+z` undo,
  `mod+shift+z` / `mod+y` redo, `Delete` / `Backspace` removes the
  selected section, `Escape` deselects, and `Arrow` / `Shift+Arrow`
  nudge the selected section by 1pt / 10pt.
- Pass a partial {@link EditorKeymap} to override individual bindings or
  register alternates (e.g. `{ redo: 'mod+r' }`).
- Pass `false` to disable the layer entirely — no window listener is
  attached.

Bindings are skipped while focus is inside an `<input>`, `<textarea>`,
`<select>`, or anything `contenteditable`, so text fields like the
document-name input remain typeable.
@example Override redo
```tsx
<Editor.Root keybindings={{ redo: 'mod+r' }}>...</Editor.Root>
```
@example Disable all shortcuts
```tsx
<Editor.Root keybindings={false}>...</Editor.Root>
```

Root.name: readOnly
Root.description:
Render the editor in read-only / viewer mode. Defaults to `false`.

When `true`:
- Section drag, resize, drop, and file upload are suppressed.
- Section floating toolbars (delete, duplicate, layer buttons) hide.
- The properties bar (document name, page size, orientation) becomes
  read-only.
- The page list hides Add Image / Add Page / per-page delete and
  ignores reorder gestures.
- The `PropertiesPanel` renders its number/text inputs disabled and
  hides the Layer buttons.
- The toolbar's `AddImageButton`, `AddTextButton`, `AddShapeButton`,
  `DrawButton`, `UndoButton`, and `RedoButton` hide themselves.
- Keybindings skip every mutating action (`undo`, `redo`,
  `deleteSection`, `nudge*`); `deselect` (Esc) still works.
- Drawing-mode pointer captures are ignored.

What still works:
- Selection (click, shift-click, marquee).
- Zoom, pan, preview dialog, print, PDF/PNG download.
@example ```tsx
<Editor.Root defaultDocument={signedContract} readOnly>
  <Editor.Properties />
  <Editor.Toolbar />
  <Editor.Canvas />
  <Editor.Preview />
</Editor.Root>
```

Root.name: showRuler
Root.description:
Render `Editor.Ruler` along the top and left edges of the canvas
viewport. Defaults to `false`. When `true`, the canvas reserves a
24px gutter on each axis so the rulers sit flush with the page edge
without occluding section geometry.

Root.name: showMinimap
Root.description:
Render `Editor.Minimap` anchored to the bottom-right of the canvas
viewport — a thumbnail of the current page plus a viewport rectangle
that pans the main canvas. Defaults to `false`.

Root.name: rulerUnit
Root.description:
Unit used by `Editor.Ruler` tick labels. Defaults to `'pt'` so the
tick values match the document's storage unit one-to-one. Set to
`'mm'` or `'in'` to surface a human-facing measurement instead.

Root.name: defaultDocument
Root.description: Uncontrolled: optional seed for the internally-owned document.

ColorPicker.name: onChange
ColorPicker.description: Fired when the user picks a swatch or commits a custom color.

ColorPicker.name: presets
ColorPicker.description:
Optional override for the swatch palette. Defaults to
{@link DEFAULT_COLOR_PRESETS} (12 colors).

ColorPicker.name: disabled
ColorPicker.description:
When `true`, the swatches and custom-color trigger become non-interactive
and visually dim. The current `value` still shows so the swatch remains
a label for the actively-selected color.

Guides.name: className
Guides.description: Optional extra className applied to the overlay container.

PageThumbnail.name: isSelected
PageThumbnail.description: Whether this page is currently selected

PageThumbnail.name: onSelect
PageThumbnail.description: Callback when the page is selected

PageThumbnail.name: onDelete
PageThumbnail.description: Callback when the page is deleted

PageThumbnail.name: dragHandlers
PageThumbnail.type: { onDragStart: (e: DragEvent<Element>) => void; onDragEnd: () => void; onDragOver: (e: DragEvent<Element>) => void; }

PageThumbnail.name: dropIndicators
PageThumbnail.description: Visual indicators for drag and drop

PageThumbnail.name: readOnly
PageThumbnail.description:
When `true`, the per-page delete button is hidden and the thumbnail
is not `draggable` (so it cannot be reordered). The thumbnail still
stays selectable. Defaults to `false`.

ContextMenu.name: children
ContextMenu.description:
Children wrapped by the right-click trigger. Typically `Editor.Canvas`
(or the page surface inside a custom shell).

ContextMenu.name: keymap
ContextMenu.description:
Override the displayed shortcut chips. Defaults to {@link DEFAULT_KEYMAP}.
Pass the same partial map you handed to `Editor.Root` `keybindings` so
the inline shortcuts match the active runtime — the menu items
themselves dispatch through {@link useEditor} actions regardless.

ContextMenu.name: className
ContextMenu.description: Optional class applied to the trigger wrapper.

useEditorKeybindings.name: deleteSection
useEditorKeybindings.description: Delete the currently selected section (no-op if none selected).

useEditorKeybindings.name: nudgeUp
useEditorKeybindings.description: Nudge the selected section up by 1pt.

useEditorKeybindings.name: nudgeDown
useEditorKeybindings.description: Nudge the selected section down by 1pt.

useEditorKeybindings.name: nudgeLeft
useEditorKeybindings.description: Nudge the selected section left by 1pt.

useEditorKeybindings.name: nudgeRight
useEditorKeybindings.description: Nudge the selected section right by 1pt.

useEditorKeybindings.name: nudgeUpLarge
useEditorKeybindings.description: Nudge the selected section up by 10pt.

useEditorKeybindings.name: nudgeDownLarge
useEditorKeybindings.description: Nudge the selected section down by 10pt.

useEditorKeybindings.name: nudgeLeftLarge
useEditorKeybindings.description: Nudge the selected section left by 10pt.

useEditorKeybindings.name: nudgeRightLarge
useEditorKeybindings.description: Nudge the selected section right by 10pt.

useEditorKeybindings.name: showHelp
useEditorKeybindings.description:
Toggle the {@link Editor.KeybindingHelp } dialog. Defaults to `mod+/` —
the same chord used by Notion, GitHub, Slack, and most other web apps
with a built-in keymap viewer. Read-only safe (the dialog is a passive
viewer).

useDocumentState.name: initialDocument
useDocumentState.description:
Optional seed document. Only read once (initial mount); subsequent
changes to this prop are ignored. Pass a new key on the parent to force
a remount if you need to swap documents.
